authoritative_sources.title: 1302.16 Attendance
authoritative_sources.publisher: HeadStart.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal attendance rule for Head Start and Early Head Start, directly authoritative for attendance recording, absence follow-up, and attendance-sensitive handling that shapes supported billing and funded-enrollment outputs.

authoritative_sources.title: 1302.18 Fees
authoritative_sources.publisher: HeadStart.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal regulation governing when Head Start programs may charge fees, making it directly authoritative for refusing unsupported family billing in Head Start-funded services.

authoritative_sources.title: 1302.15 Enrollment
authoritative_sources.publisher: HeadStart.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal funded-enrollment rule that defines how active enrollment and slot usage constrain attendance-driven revenue and leakage handling in Head Start environments.

authoritative_sources.title: Addressing Vacant Slots Due to Chronic Absenteeism in Head Start Programs
authoritative_sources.publisher: HeadStart.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official OHS instruction clarifying how chronic absenteeism, vacant slots, and refill timing affect funded enrollment and revenue exception handling.

authoritative_sources.title: Improving Child Care Access, Affordability, and Stability in the Child Care and Development Fund (CCDF)
authoritative_sources.publisher: Federal Register / Administration for Children and Families, Office of Child Care
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal CCDF rule baseline for provider payment practices and family co-payment policy, directly shaping subsidy-funded attendance and billing workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Charge and subsidy payment support review

authoritative_sources.title: Child Care Financial Assistance Options
authoritative_sources.publisher: ChildCare.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Office of Child Care guidance distinguishing funding pathways such as subsidy, voucher, Head Start, and private-pay care, which determines what source records must exist before billing recommendations are safe.

authoritative_sources.title: CCDF Family Co-Payments
authoritative_sources.publisher: Child Care Technical Assistance Network / Office of Child Care
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Office of Child Care technical assistance directly tied to CCDF administration and provider/family billing operations, making it a recognized workflow reference for co-payment handling.

authoritative_sources.title: Error Rate Review and Improper Payments Resources
authoritative_sources.publisher: Child Care Technical Assistance Network / Office of Child Care
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Office of Child Care control guidance focused on improper payments and error-rate review, directly relevant to denial handling, unsupported adjustments, and reconciliation controls.

authoritative_sources.title: Licensing Regulations Database
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ACF's public licensing database is the authoritative retrieval path for state attendance-record and provider-operation rules that can materially change what attendance evidence is sufficient.

authoritative_sources.title: Child Care Automated Reporting System (CARS) Training and Technical Assistance
authoritative_sources.publisher: Administration for Children and Families, Office of Child Care
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official OCC systems reference identifying child care reporting structures and subsidy-reporting context, which helps define authoritative system-of-record expectations for public-program payment handling.

workflow_stages.name: Funding-path classification and attendance intake
workflow_stages.description: Identify whether the case is Head Start, CCDF subsidy, private pay, or mixed funding and confirm which attendance records and calendars govern the request.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Charge, family-fee, and posting support validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate that attendance events, authorization spans, family fee or co-payment rules, and program-specific billing constraints support a charge or posting outcome.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Remittance, denial, and reconciliation review
workflow_stages.description: Compare attendance, billing, family-ledger, and remittance records to identify matched items, denials, unsupported days, and unresolved variances.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception handling and closeout
workflow_stages.description: Package denials, disputes, chronic-absence leakage, unsupported adjustments, and mixed-funding conflicts for the correct owner instead of making unsupported corrections.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Boundary and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when accounting, tax, legal, subsidy-award, or jurisdiction-specific payment-rule authority is required or when public sources conflict with tenant rules.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Absence reason and attendance exception notes
Family fee schedules and co-payment records
Private-pay tuition schedules and family account statements
Head Start funded-enrollment and attendance follow-up records
Subsidy claims, remittance files, and denial notices
Tenant billing policy extracts and approval matrices
systems_of_record: Child-care management or attendance capture systems
State or local subsidy portals and remittance systems
Head Start attendance and funded-enrollment tracking systems
Document repositories for authorizations and fee records
Exception, denial, and reconciliation work queues

allowed: Validate whether retrieved attendance, authorization, fee, and remittance records support billing, posting, reconciliation, or escalation outputs.
Prepare traceable summaries, reconciliation notes, and exception packets with explicit uncertainty and owner routing.
Separate mixed-funding child-care streams when public rules clearly prohibit combined financial treatment.
escalate: Missing jurisdiction-specific subsidy manual or absent-day policy changes the billing outcome
Attendance, authorization, and remittance records disagree
Denial, dispute, or variance requires program-finance, subsidy-admin, or policy-owner review
Head Start fee rules and tenant billing requests conflict
refuse: Requests to create or alter attendance facts without source evidence
Requests for write-offs, tax treatment, accounting-signoff, or subsidy-eligibility determinations
Requests to treat the child-care industry label as cosmetic and skip funding-path or attendance-record context

domain_failure_modes: Billing a child-care service day without support from attendance and authorization records
Treating Head Start, CCDF, and private-pay rules as interchangeable and generating unsupported family charges
Silently correcting denials or remittance mismatches without traceable source evidence
Proceeding when jurisdiction-specific absent-day or provider-payment rules are missing
Using generic billing terminology that hides child-care artifacts such as sign-in/out logs, voucher spans, or funded-enrollment context

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Source records do not support the requested billing or adjustment
Reconciliation mismatch or denial requires exception handling
Stakeholder asks for an unsupported financial override

unresolved_ambiguities.detail: State and local subsidy manuals can materially change whether payment is attendance-based, enrollment-based, or subject to capped absent-day handling. The package therefore requires tenant retrieval of the governing jurisdiction manual before decisive billing output.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: CCDF payment-practice baseline under active policy change
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: The federal CCDF final rule published on March 1, 2024 is the current public baseline used here. If a later final federal or state action changes provider-payment expectations after April 9, 2026, the lane must refresh sources and escalate when tenant implementation status is unclear.
